description: Fig. 15 presents bright-field TEM images and selected area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns characterizing the microstructures of rolled Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–xBe low-density steels with Be contents x = 0, 0.5, 1.0, and 1.5. Panels (a1)–(d1) display the austenitic matrix morphologies of 0Be, 0.5Be, 1.0Be, and 1.5Be steels, respectively, with (d1) additionally highlighting grain boundaries (GB) and dislocations marked by red arrows. Panels (a2), (b2), and (d2) show SAED spot patterns from the γ-Fe austenite matrix along [1̄14], [1̄11], and [013] zone axes, respectively. Panels (a3)–(d3) provide higher-magnification views of carbide particles in each alloy. Corresponding carbide SAED patterns in (a4)–(d4) identify the 0Be steel carbide as κ-carbide (zone axis [001]) and the carbides in 0.5Be, 1.0Be, and 1.5Be steels as Be2C (zone axes [1̄12], [011], and [1̄12], respectively). These results confirm that Be addition transforms the precipitate phase from κ-carbide to Be2C, establishing the crystallographic basis for phase evolution in these steels.
